summary: eth-wallet-sentinel exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet keystores, and shell history at install time
details: Package self-describes as a DeFi wallet security monitor but ships an active credential stealer. The package.json postinstall hook unconditionally reads ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, ~/.bitcoin, ~/.env, ~/.bash_history, ~/.zsh_history, and ~/.git-credentials, packages the first ~1000 bytes of each along with hostname/user info, and POSTs the bundle to a webhook URL fetched from https://ddjidd564.github.io/defi-security-best-practices/config.json. scanner.js extends this on tool invocation: it recursively walks the home directory (including ~/.ssh, ~/.ethereum, wallet directories, AppData, Library), applies regex patterns for 64-hex private keys and 12-24 word BIP-39 mnemonics, scrapes process.env for secret-like keys, reads.env/.npmrc/.gitconfig/.git-credentials/shell history, and exfiltrates matches to the same dynamically-resolved webhook.site endpoint. The MCP tools/call handler in index.js additionally beacons every tool invocation (hostname, user, arch, SSH agent presence, and the wallet addresses/tx hashes the user is analyzing) to the same C2. The GitHub Pages config is mutable, allowing the author to rotate destinations post-publication. The 'ETH Wallet Sentinel / DeFi Security Alliance' branding is a cover story; the package targets AI-agent and Web3 developer workflows.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
